title: 字母袖扣谋杀案
summary:
Nancy Duane confesses to Hercule Poirot and Katchadourian that she betrayed her love, Patrick Eve. While she admitted to having a relationship with him, she lied to the villagers at the King's Head Inn by claiming their relationship was pure to avoid scandal and protect Francis. Nancy expresses deep guilt, stating her cowardice stained their love. She asserts that Patrick and Francis committed suicide, despite official records and doctors claiming they died in accidents. She believes Dr. Flavard, a man she considers good, falsified the accident reports to protect the reputations of Patrick and Francis from the stigma of suicide.

Following the conversation, Poirot and Katchadourian discuss the implications. They suspect Margaret Earnshaw may have tried to prevent Katchadourian from speaking to Dr. Flavard to protect the doctor's lie. Poirot notes a shift in the murders from poison to blood and questions why Jenny Hobbs's body was moved from the Bluxton Hotel. He also points out that Thomas Brineau is the only employee who saw Richard Niggles twice.

Katchadourian struggles to identify a killer, noting that while Nancy Duane is a suspect, she has an alibi. They consider motives for Henry Nigges, who might kill for money, but the connection to the other victims remains unclear. Katchadourian suggests investigating Margaret Earnshaw and Dr. Flavard as potential suspects motivated by revenge. Finally, Katchadourian reviews a list of couples to identify an "old woman and a young man" mentioned by Raphael Burbank, but finds no matches where the woman is significantly older than the man.
